summary: A small rectangular single-story building with stone-brick lower walls, a cobblestone perimeter foundation/walk, and a dark red stepped gable-like roof made of nether-brick (dark planks). The long sides have repeated glass windows set into stonebrick wall sections; stone slabs accent the roof ridge and gable ends. The structure sits recessed into flattened dirt around it.

shape.overall_form: long rectangular box with a raised stepped roof centered along the long axis
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along the long central axis

elements: rows of 3-4 glass windows along each long side set into stonebrick
cobblestone surround one block out from wall forming a low parapet/foundation
nether-brick stepped roof with stone slab caps at three stepped positions on each gable end
single-story interior (no visible second floor or windows above)
rebuild_hints: Base footprint is a centered rectangle with a one-block wide cobblestone ring offset from the stonebrick walls by one block.
Stonebrick walls are approximately 3 blocks tall above ground before the roof starts; place windows as 1x1 panes repeating along long sides separated by 1 stonebrick column.

Build a nether-brick slab/step roof: a flat central run of nether-brick blocks spanning the long axis with two stepped layers down each side; add stone slab blocks as decorative caps at the gable ends and along the short axis center.
Top-down view: roof occupies the interior rectangle; cobblestone ring is aligned around it leaving a one-block dirt trench outside.
uncertainties: Exact block types: nether_brick identified from color but could be dark wood variant; confidence high but not certain.
Precise interior layout and presence/location of a door not visible in all views (one image hints at a wooden door on one short side).
Exact dimensions in blocks are estimated from pixel views; width/depth/height may be off by a few blocks.
